name: Pre-Separation Checklist
description: Complete timeline of actions to take 12, 6, and 3 months before separation. Covers medical records, VA registration, financial planning, and career preparation.

name: Transition Assistance Program (TAP)
description: DoD-mandated transition program providing career readiness, financial planning, and VA benefits briefings. Required for all separating service members.

name: DD-214 — Secure Your Copy
description: Your DD-214 is the single most important document after separation. Request certified copies before you leave. Needed for every VA benefit, employer verification, and state program.

name: VA Healthcare Enrollment
description: Enroll in VA healthcare before you lose TRICARE. Most veterans are eligible. Apply online — takes about 30 minutes. Don't wait until you need it.

name: VA Disability Claim — File Early
description: File your disability claim before or immediately after separation. Benefits Decision Letter (BDD) claims can be filed 180-90 days before separation for faster processing.

name: GI Bill — Choose Your Chapter
description: Understand which GI Bill chapter you qualify for (Post-9/11, Montgomery, Chapter 31 VR&E). Each has different benefits, BAH rates, and eligibility requirements.

name: SkillBridge — Start While Still Serving
description: DoD program that lets you intern at civilian companies during your last 180 days of service while still receiving military pay. Hundreds of employers participate.

name: Vocational Rehabilitation (VR&E / Chapter 31)
description: If you have a service-connected disability, VR&E provides career counseling, training, education, and job placement. Can pay for certifications, tools, and even self-employment startup costs.

name: Resume Translation — Military to Civilian
description: Translate your military experience, MOS, and accomplishments into civilian-friendly resume language. Use the AAAI Resume Builder template or work with Hire Heroes USA.

name: Credential & License Transfer
description: Many military skills have direct civilian equivalents. Most states offer expedited licensing for veterans. Check your MOS-to-civilian credential pathway.

name: Veterans Preference in Federal Hiring
description: Veterans get 5-10 point hiring preference for federal jobs. Disabled veterans get additional priority. USAJobs is the primary portal.

name: Hiring Our Heroes Corporate Fellowship
description: 12-week fellowship program matching transitioning service members with Fortune 500 companies. Fully paid while still on active duty. One of the highest-placement transition programs.

name: VA Home Loan — Zero Down Mortgage
description: The VA loan benefit requires zero down payment, no PMI, and offers competitive rates. One of the most powerful financial tools for transitioning veterans.

name: SSVF — Supportive Services for Veteran Families
description: Temporary financial assistance for rent, utilities, and deposits. Available to very low-income veteran families at risk of homelessness.

name: Build Your Post-Military Budget
description: Military pay includes BAH, BAS, and tax advantages that disappear at separation. Build a realistic civilian budget accounting for taxes, healthcare costs, and housing.

name: TSP — Don't Cash Out
description: Your Thrift Savings Plan follows you after separation. Roll it to an IRA or leave it in TSP. Cashing out triggers taxes + 10% penalty. Protect your retirement savings.

name: SCRA Protections — Know When They End
description: Servicemembers Civil Relief Act protections (6% interest cap, lease termination, foreclosure protection) expire after separation. Plan accordingly.

name: State Veterans Benefits — Register in Your State
description: Every state offers unique veteran benefits: property tax exemptions, education waivers, employment preference, hunting/fishing licenses. Register with your state VA office.

name: Veteran ID Card — Get Yours
description: The Veteran Health Identification Card (VHIC) or VA-issued Veteran ID Card (VIC) provides proof of service for discounts, benefits, and identification.

name: Military Discounts — Immediate Savings
description: Hundreds of retailers, restaurants, telecom providers, and service companies offer military discounts. Start saving on day one.

name: Mental Health — Don't Wait for a Crisis
description: Transition is one of the highest-risk periods for veterans. VA mental health services are available to all enrolled veterans. Vet Centers offer readjustment counseling with no enrollment required.

name: Vet Centers — Free Readjustment Counseling
description: 300+ Vet Centers nationwide offer free counseling for combat veterans, sexual trauma survivors, and bereaved families. No VA enrollment required.

name: Veteran Service Organizations (VSOs)
description: Join a VSO like DAV, VFW, or American Legion. They provide free claims assistance, community, advocacy, and support networks. Your strongest ally in navigating the VA system.

name: Family Support During Transition
description: Transition affects the whole family. Spouses need career support, children need school transitions, and everyone needs a support network. Connect with family programs.

name: Hidden Benefits You're Probably Missing
description: Most veterans only claim 3-4 benefits. There are dozens more: clothing allowance, commissary access, national park passes, tax exemptions, insurance programs, and more.
